verdicts.note: The record claims exists='yes' and provides a URL to the Pumble Node SDK manifest documentation (https://cake-com.github.io/pumble-node-sdk/manifest.html). However, this is NOT a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server—it is simply the documentation for Pumble's own app manifest/SDK framework. The evidence contains no mention of an MCP server for Pumble. The URL points to Pumble's own developer documentation, not a separate MCP integration.
The URL provided (https://cake-com.github.io/pumble-node-sdk/manifest.html) is to Pumble's own SDK documentation for building Pumble apps, not an MCP server. This should be removed.

Evidence confirms OAuth2 flow (https://cake-com.github.io/pumble-node-sdk/authorization.html) and API key generation via the Pumble API addon (https://pumble.com/help/integrations/automation-workflow-integrations/api-keys-integration/). Both methods are accurately described.

Evidence confirms that free plan limits integrations to specific native apps (Clockify, Plaky) per https://pumble.com/learn/pumble/free-plan-explained/, and full API/integration access requires paid Pro or Business plans per https://pumble.com/learn/pumble/business-plan-explained/. 'paid-plan' is correct.

Evidence shows REST API with Node SDK (pumble-node-sdk) covering messages, channels, files, reactions, scheduled messages, etc. per https://cake-com.github.io/pumble-node-sdk/api-client.html. Type 'REST' and breadth 'medium' are reasonable.

Buildability 'buildable-with-caveats' is accurate given that full API access requires paid plans and OAuth2 app creation involves an app manifest/registration process rather than instant key generation.
Main blocker accurately reflects that full API/integration access is gated behind paid plans and OAuth2 app creation requires going through an app manifest/marketplace process.
The one_liner accurately summarizes Pumble's offering: team messaging with REST API and Node SDK, OAuth2 and API key auth, and integration access gated by paid plans.
Confidence of 0.6 is reasonable given the evidence provided covers the key claims but some details about the exact OAuth2 app registration process and free-tier limitations are inferred.

revised.name: Pumble
revised.one_liner: Pumble is a team messaging platform offering a REST API (with Node SDK) supporting OAuth2 app tokens or workspace-generated API keys for bots/messages/channels, with integration access gated by paid plans.

auth_methods.notes: OAuth2 flow via consent screen and POST https://api-ga.pumble.com/oauth2/access to exchange code for access token, used for full apps.
Pumble API addon allows generating API keys/tokens directly within a workspace (x-app-token + token headers) for simpler direct requests.

revised.self_serve_notes: Free tier exists but third-party app/integration access is restricted to a limited set of native integrations (e.g., Clockify, Plaky); full API/app integration access requires upgrading to Pro or Business plan.

api_surface.notes: REST API with SDK (pumble-node-sdk) covering messages, channels, files, reactions, scheduled messages, etc. Also has an app manifest/trigger framework for building integrations.

revised.main_blocker: Full API/integration access is gated behind paid Pro/Business plans, and OAuth2 app creation requires going through Pumble's app manifest/marketplace process rather than instant self-serve key generation.

evidence.supports: Describes x-app-token and token headers required for every API request, and lists API methods/scopes.
Details OAuth2 authorization flow for obtaining user/bot access tokens.
Describes the Pumble API addon allowing users to generate API keys directly within a workspace.
Confirms free plan limits integrations to specific native apps like Clockify and Plaky.
Confirms unlimited/full integration access requires paid Business or Pro plan.
Describes the app manifest and SDK framework used to build Pumble apps/integrations.

flags: Integration/API access restricted by pricing tier
OAuth2 app creation process not fully self-serve instant (requires app registration via manifest)
No official MCP server found—only Pumble's own SDK/app framework documented
